data.narrative:
eleQtron is a Series A quantum computing company founded by Jan Leisse that has raised €50M+ to date. The quantum computing sector comprises a sparse comparable universe of 3 public companies (IONQ, RGTI, QBTS) and 8 private peers including IonQ, Quantinuum, Rigetti Computing, D-Wave Quantum, and Infleqtion.

The peer-median valuation method is not applicable to this analysis. The quantum computing sector contains only 7 same-sector peers overall, but filtering for same-stage companies, adjacent-stage companies, or similar valuation tier yielded fewer than 3 datapoints in each category—below the threshold required for reliable median-based triangulation. An all-sector median would introduce excessive noise given the heterogeneity in business models, technology maturity, and commercialization timelines across the comparable set.

Without a sufficiently dense peer cohort at the same stage and valuation tier, traditional relative-value methods cannot be applied with confidence. Valuation of eleQtron would require alternative approaches such as venture capital method modeling, sum-of-the-parts analysis, or discounted cash flow projection. The primary valuation uncertainty stems from the thinness of the private quantum computing peer set at Series A stage and the nascency of public quantum computing valuations, which limits the evidentiary weight of any single comparable. Additional data on eleQtron's technology differentiation, customer pipeline, and burn rate would be necessary to ground a defensible valuation range.
